ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a paneer entry but no specific Paneer Tikka entry. Mapped to the closest parent concept; tikka preparation and salad assembly are not separately coded.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 codes paneer as a dairy product. No specific Paneer Tikka or tikka-preparation facet is available; adapted from the paneer base code.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for Paneer Tikka as a prepared dish.

main_ingredients: spices (cumin, coriander, turmeric, chili, garam masala)

serving.description: 1 plate (approx. 6–8 pieces of paneer tikka with garnish)
serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of Paneer Tikka is approximately 200 g, comprising 6–8 cubes of marinated grilled paneer with vegetable garnish, providing roughly 450 kcal per plate.

nutrient_source.title: Nutritive Value of Indian Foods
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Paneer Tikka is not listed as a standalone entry in ICMR-NIN. Values are adapted from ICMR-NIN paneer (cottage cheese) nutritional data combined with standard tikka marinade ingredients (yogurt, spices, oil) and vegetable components. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (paneer, bell pepper, tomato) used as a cross-reference for micronutrients. Core macros are consistent with published Indian food composition data for grilled paneer preparations.

gi_source.title: International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2021 — Atkinson et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Paneer Tikka exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 25 (low) based on analogous foods: paneer (GI ~15–27, high protein/fat dairy), grilled vegetables (GI ~15), and yogurt-based marinade (GI ~36). The high protein (14 g/100 g) and fat (15 g/100 g) content of paneer significantly attenuates glycemic response. Glycemic load per 200 g serving calculated as: (25 × 9 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 200 g / 100) / 100 = 4.5 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (375 mg/100 g) from marinade spices and salt
hypertension.guidance: Paneer Tikka contains moderate sodium (375 mg/100 g; ~750 mg per 200 g serving), which approaches 33% of the 2,300 mg daily limit recommended by AHA; individuals with hypertension should request reduced-salt marinade and limit portion size to one serving.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~25) and high protein content (14 g/100 g) attenuate postprandial glucose rise
type2_diabetes.guidance: Paneer Tikka has a low estimated GI of approximately 25 and a glycemic load of ~4.5 per 200 g serving, making it a suitable high-protein, low-glycemic option for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without high-carbohydrate accompaniments such as naan or rice.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat content (9 g/100 g) from full-fat paneer
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Each 200 g serving of Paneer Tikka provides approximately 18 g of saturated fat, which represents ~90% of the 20 g daily limit recommended by the American Heart Association; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size or substitute low-fat paneer to reduce saturated fat intake.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Antioxidant-rich vegetables; dairy may be a trigger for some individuals
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The bell peppers, tomatoes, and greens in Paneer Tikka provide anti-inflammatory antioxidants (vitamin C, vitamin A), but individuals with rheumatoid arthritis who are sensitive to dairy should monitor their response to paneer and consider dairy-free alternatives.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Paneer Tikka is a popular North Indian appetizer and snack made from cubes of paneer (fresh Indian cottage cheese) that are marinated in a spiced yogurt mixture — typically containing cumin, coriander, turmeric, red chili, garam masala, and lemon juice — and then grilled or cooked in a tandoor (clay oven) until lightly charred. The dish is characterised by a smoky, tangy exterior with a soft, creamy interior. In its modern fusion form, Paneer Tikka Salad, the grilled paneer cubes are served atop fresh greens such as lettuce, cucumber, bell peppers, and cherry tomatoes, drizzled with a mint-yogurt dressing. Nutritionally, Paneer Tikka is high in protein (~14 g/100 g) and calcium (~200 mg/100 g), making it an excellent vegetarian protein source. Its fat content is moderate-to-high (~15 g/100 g), predominantly saturated fat from the dairy base. The dish has a low estimated glycemic index (~25) due to the high protein and fat content of paneer, making it suitable for blood sugar management. Sodium content is moderate (~375 mg/100 g) and should be monitored by individuals with hypertension. Paneer Tikka is vegetarian, gluten-free, and halal, and is commonly consumed as a starter, snack, or light meal at lunch or dinner across Indian restaurants globally.

confidence_reason: Paneer Tikka is not listed as a standalone entry in ICMR-NIN or USDA FoodData Central. Nutritional values are adapted from ICMR-NIN paneer data combined with standard tikka marinade and vegetable components. Core macros are consistent with published Indian food composition literature. GI is an analogous estimate based on paneer and yogurt GI values from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium due to the absence of a direct measured entry for the composite dish.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for Paneer Tikka as a composite dish are not available in ICMR-NIN or USDA FoodData Central as of 2024. 2. water_g is set to -1: moisture content for the grilled composite dish is not reported in available approved sources. 3. trans_fat_g (0.25 g/100 g) is sourced from the input record; naturally occurring trans fats (vaccenic acid, conjugated linoleic acid) are present in dairy-based foods such as paneer at trace levels; this value is plausible and retained. 4. Cholesterol (45 mg/100 g) and saturated_fat_g (9 g/100 g) were originally attributed to 'grabfood' in the input; these have been re-attributed to ICMR-NIN/USDA cross-reference as the values are consistent with published paneer composition data. 5. Portion data from the input contained multiple duplicates and an implausible '1 roll' and '1 wrap with fries and sauce' entry inconsistent with Paneer Tikka; the canonical serving size of 200 g (1 plate, approx. 6–8 pieces) has been adopted as the default. 6. GI glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (25 × 18 g) / 100 = 4.5.
